portfolio_context.thesis: Auto-parts turnaround with operating leverage and margin recovery potential
portfolio_context.prior_review: 2026-03-07 v5: HOLD, recommended revising target from $193 to ~$92

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Are catalysts showing up on schedule?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: MIXED. FY2025 Adj EBITDA improved to $209.7M (7.6% margin) from $180.7M, but Q4 2025 margins regressed to 5.2% due to customer production disruptions and inflation. FCF dropped to $16.3M from $25.9M. 2026 guidance targets $280M Adj EBITDA (~10% margin) which would be a significant step-up. Debt refinancing completed March 2026, extending maturities to 2031 -- a major deleveraging catalyst achieved.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Is management saying one thing, doing another?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: LARGELY CONSISTENT. CEO Edwards (tenure since 2012) delivered on organizational restructuring (product-line focus from 2024), China growth pivot (74% of new business wins EV-related, targeting 60%+ China OEM share by 2030), and debt refinancing. However, Q4 2025 margin miss vs full-year trajectory raises execution questions for 2026 ramp.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Are margins shifting unexpectedly?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: IMPROVING TREND WITH SETBACK. Full-year gross margin improved from 5.1% (FY2022) to 11.7% (FY2025), but the Q4 dip (5.2% Adj EBITDA margin vs 8.2% prior year Q4) signals vulnerability to volume/mix headwinds. Targeted 10%+ Adj EBITDA margin for FY2026 is ambitious given the Q4 trajectory.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Are competitors pulling ahead?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: NO MATERIAL DRIFT. CPS retains #1 global position in sealing systems, #2 in fuel/brake delivery, #3 in fluid transfer. 8.3% market share in auto parts. $298M new business wins in 2025 with 74% EV/hybrid-related shows positioning for industry transition.

thesis_integrity_checklist.question: Which macro events matter?
thesis_integrity_checklist.answer: US light vehicle sales projected to decline to 15.6M in 2026 (from 16.1M in 2025). BEV adoption stagnating but hybrid growing. China OEM growth (15%+ CAGR 2025-2028) is a meaningful tailwind. Tariff/trade policy risk exists but not yet triggered for auto parts. Global light vehicle production growth flat to slightly negative in 2026.

catalyst_tracking.evidence: FY2025 Adj EBITDA margin 7.6% (up from 6.6% in FY2024), but Q4 regressed to 5.2%. 2026 target is 10%+ margin. Gross margin improved from 5.1% (FY2022) to 11.7% (FY2025). Operating income turned positive at $107M in FY2025.
catalyst_tracking.verdict: Progress visible on annual basis but Q4 setback tempers confidence. 10% EBITDA target for 2026 is achievable if volume headwinds stabilize.

catalyst_tracking.evidence: Organizational restructuring to product-line focus complete. CEO Edwards in place since 2012 providing continuity. New business wins of $298M in 2025. China strategy executing with 36% China revenue targeting 60%+ by 2030.
catalyst_tracking.verdict: Management executing on strategic initiatives. Long CEO tenure provides stability.

catalyst_tracking.evidence: March 2026 refinancing issued $1.1B in 9.25% secured notes due 2031, retiring all 2026-2027 maturities. Near-term liquidity risk eliminated. Target net leverage 2x by 2028. S&P expects adjusted debt/EBITDA of 5.4x in 2026.
catalyst_tracking.verdict: Major positive catalyst achieved. Maturity wall cleared. High coupon (9.25%) constrains FCF but survival risk substantially reduced.

catalyst_tracking.evidence: China revenue from Chinese OEMs at 36%, targeting 60%+ by 2030. Total China revenue projected 15%+ CAGR 2025-2028. Total sales to Chinese OEMs globally expected to triple over 5 years.
catalyst_tracking.verdict: New catalyst not in original thesis. Potentially significant revenue diversification and growth driver.

management_consistency.what_management_said: CEO Edwards committed to margin recovery through organizational restructuring, cost optimization, and pivot to EV/hybrid content. Targeted deleveraging to 2x net leverage by 2028. Guided for positive FCF and improving EBITDA margins.
management_consistency.what_management_did: Delivered product-line reorganization in 2024. Completed $1.1B debt refinancing in March 2026 extending maturities to 2031. Adj EBITDA improved from $180.7M to $209.7M (FY2024 to FY2025). Generated positive FCF in FY2024 and FY2025. Won $298M new business with 74% EV-related. However, Q4 2025 showed margin regression and missed analyst EPS estimates.
management_consistency.match_mismatch: MATCH on strategic execution and deleveraging. PARTIAL MISMATCH on near-term margin consistency -- Q4 2025 was a setback against the improving trend.

margin_competitor_drift.margin_change: Significant improvement: Gross margin from 5.1% (FY2022) to 11.7% (FY2025). Adj EBITDA margin from 0.02% (FY2022) to 7.6% (FY2025). However, still well below pre-COVID levels (18.5% gross margin FY2017). Q4 2025 regression (5.2% Adj EBITDA) is a concern.
margin_competitor_drift.competitor_drift: CPS maintains #1 sealing, #2 fuel/brake, #3 fluid transfer globally. 8.3% market share. No competitor displacement evident. EV content positioning (74% of new wins) demonstrates adaptation to industry transition.
open_risks: Negative equity (-$83.5M) persists -- probability ceiling of 60% triggered
Total debt of $1.26B with 9.25% coupon = ~$102M annual interest, consuming most operating cash flow
US light vehicle sales projected to decline in 2026 (15.6M vs 16.1M in 2025)
Q4 2025 margin regression may indicate structural headwinds not fully resolved
Heavy OEM customer concentration (Ford, GM, Stellantis) creates dependency risk

base_case_assumptions: FCF margin: 4.5% (10% Adj EBITDA target minus ~3.5% capex minus ~2% interest = ~4.5% FCF margin on optimistic read; conservative given $16.3M FCF on $2.74B = 0.59% current)
FCF multiple: 10x (cyclical/industrial baseline 12-15x minus -3x leverage discount minus -2x negative equity risk = ~7-10x; using top of discounted range given debt maturity extension)

worst_case_floor.summary: Negative FCF margin trough produces negative floor price, implying equity wipeout risk in worst case. Downside scored at 100% (capped). Negative equity (-$83.5M) and TBV/share of -$14.65 confirm structural solvency risk. However, March 2026 debt refinancing extended all maturities to 2031, substantially reducing near-term survival risk.

trough_path.data_point: Industrial 12-15x baseline minus -3x leverage, -2x negative equity, -2x declining FCF trend = 5-8x; used 6x

worst_case_floor.downside_normalization: Mechanical floor -$71.22 (downside 322.56%) -> scored at 100% cap. Negative equity + negative trough FCF = equity wipeout scenario plausible. TBV/share -$14.65 confirms negative asset backing.

optional_scenarios.name: Bull case: Management delivers 2026 targets
optional_scenarios.takeaway: If CPS achieves 10% Adj EBITDA margin and 3% revenue growth in 2026 ($2.82B revenue, ~6% FCF margin, 12x multiple as deleveraging progresses), target ~$113. At $32 over 2 years = ~88% CAGR. This requires margin trajectory to accelerate materially from Q4 2025 levels.

optional_scenarios.name: Bear case: Q4 2025 regression continues
optional_scenarios.takeaway: If margins stagnate at 2025 levels (7.6% Adj EBITDA, ~0.6% FCF margin) and auto production declines, FCF stays around $15-20M. Stock trades at 8-10x OCF = $29-36. Limited downside from $32 given refinancing removed near-term debt wall, but no meaningful upside either.

sell_trigger_check.name: T1: Target reached / forward <30%
sell_trigger_check.evidence: Forward CAGR 49.01% well above 30% hurdle. Stock at $32 vs refreshed target $71.05.

sell_trigger_check.name: T2: Rapid move / forward <10-15%
sell_trigger_check.evidence: Forward CAGR 49.01% well above rapid-move guardrail. No rapid price appreciation occurred.

sell_trigger_check.name: T3: Fundamental thesis break
sell_trigger_check.evidence: Thesis is operating leverage + margin recovery turnaround. Margin recovery is progressing (gross margin 5.1% -> 11.7% over 3 years) though slower than hoped. Debt refinancing in March 2026 removed the biggest existential risk (2027 maturity wall). CEO Edwards stable since 2012. No thesis break -- but negative equity and high interest burden constrain the recovery path.

holding_verdict.reason: CPS thesis is intact but execution is slower than originally projected. The March 2026 debt refinancing was a major positive, eliminating the 2027 maturity wall and extending to 2031. Margin recovery is trending in the right direction on a multi-year basis (gross margin 5.1% to 11.7%), but Q4 2025 regression and declining FCF trend ($36.5M -> $25.9M -> $16.3M) require close monitoring. Forward CAGR of 49% from today's price provides significant margin of safety. However, negative equity (-$83.5M), high interest burden ($102M/year at 9.25%), and the 100% mechanical downside warrant MONITOR status rather than pure HOLD. The 4.5% FCF margin assumption is optimistic given current run-rate of 0.59% -- the thesis depends entirely on margin expansion materializing in 2026-2027.
monitor_items: Q1 2026 Adj EBITDA margin -- must show improvement from Q4 2025 dip (5.2%) toward 10% full-year target
FCF trend reversal -- FY2025 FCF of $16.3M was lower than FY2024 ($25.9M); needs to inflect upward
Equity position trajectory -- negative equity of -$83.5M; watch for retained earnings improvement as net losses narrow
Net leverage ratio progress toward 2x target by 2028 (currently ~5.4x estimated)
China OEM revenue contribution -- tracking toward 60%+ share and 15% CAGR
Auto production volumes -- US light vehicle sales projected to decline in 2026

sources: FMP Stable API (profile, income, balance, cashflow, ratios, metrics, price-history)
